steps.title: Read the intersection from the graph
steps.content: The solution to a graphed system is the point that lies on both lines. One line is horizontal at $y=3$, so the solution must have $y=3$. The slanted line crosses that horizontal line at $x=4$.

steps.title: State the ordered pair
steps.content: Putting the coordinates together gives the intersection point $$(4,3).$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B is $(4,3)$, which is the point where the two graphed lines meet.
